items.q: How do you give advice with 'il faut'?
items.correct: Il faut + infinitive, e.g. 'il faut manger des fruits'
items.why: Students conjugate after 'il faut'; it is followed by the infinitive to express what one must do.
Students mistranslate it; 'équilibré' means balanced, central to healthy-living vocabulary.

items.q: Which is part of a healthy lifestyle?
items.why: Students pick unhealthy options; regular exercise supports wellbeing, the focus of this theme.

items.q: Which sentence gives healthy-living advice correctly?
items.correct: Il faut manger des fruits et des légumes.
items.why: It's Il faut manger des fruits et des légumes.. “Il faut mange des fruits.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Wellbeing. “A bank” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
